meaning: vt.使缠上,纠缠,卷入,连累,使混乱
examples: Bureaucracy can entangle applications for months.
Elephants sometimes entangle their noses together as a hug, showing greetings and admiration.
EU, entangle investors in red tape and will deter many from setting up shop in South Africa.
Bags and small plastic pieces can entangle marine animals causing them to drown.
